key_insight: VRR-Stop decides whether to keep repairing or stop by estimating the true marginal gain of a noisy verify-repair loop, and it switches to a conservative fallback when verification becomes too unreliable.
engineering_relevance: For LLM agents that generate code, solve math, or use tools, this gives a principled stopping rule that can reduce wasted repair steps and prevent good solutions from being damaged by over-repair.
method: Models verification and repair with four noise parameters: false accept, false reject, repair improvement, and repair damage.
Uses belief filtering over repeated verifier votes to estimate committed validity and decide based on the sign of the true marginal gain.
Adds VRR-Guard as an estimation-free fallback when verifier discrimination is near zero or stopping calibration becomes unreliable.
evidence_signal: On a GSM8K stress test, VRR-Stop improved final true validity by 60.6 percentage points over fixed five-round repair while using only 0.72 repair rounds on average.
watch_item: The method depends on verifier discrimination and a reliable decision margin; when the verifier is nearly uninformative, estimation error can flip the stopping decision.
paper_title: Verify, Repair, Repeat, or Stop? Robust Stopping for Noisy Verify-Repair Loops in LLM Agents
